J1.basis: InboxZap (email cleanup tool) pivoted to Lumina (AI meeting assistant), but the migration was left half-done in code. GSC data showed *all 18 homepage clicks* were brand searches for "inboxzap"... while 12 legacy email-cleanup blog posts (still pulling ~15k impressions) had become orphaned
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, distinguishes live product (Lumina meeting assistant) from legacy GSC demand (inbox-cleanup brand + blog impressions), and frames every recommendation around that reality; the only gap is treating legacy demand as fully disposable rather than a bridging asset.

J2.basis: Retired the 12 off-topic legacy posts: 301'd them to /blog in next.config.js instead of leaving them 404-bound or orphaned — preserves whatever link equity remains rather than losing it outright.
J2.rationale: One blanket disposition (all 12 → 301 to /blog) with an aggregate ~15k-impressions justification and equity reasoning, so above anchor_0, but no asset-by-asset or tiered treatment and no per-post impressions/positions from the export.

J3.basis: 301'd them to /blog... preserves whatever link equity remains... Seeded 4 new posts genuinely on-topic for Lumina... to start building topical authority in the new niche
J3.rationale: Lands a defensible middle path — redirect to preserve equity, build the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine, and answer 'What happened to InboxZap?' as a bridge — but the redirect target is the blog index rather than relevant successors and the tension is implied rather than explicitly articulated.

J1.basis: InboxZap (email cleanup tool) pivoted to Lumina (AI meeting assistant), but the migration was left half-done in code. GSC data showed *all 18 homepage clicks* were brand searches for "inboxzap"... 12 legacy email-cleanup blog posts (still pulling ~15k impressions) had become orphaned
J1.rationale: Explicitly names the pivot, correctly reads the live site as the meeting assistant, GSC as legacy/brand demand, and frames every fix around that reality — near-textbook anchor_10; docks one point only because it doesn't distinguish which repo assets were stale vs current as sharply.

J2.basis: Retired the 12 off-topic legacy posts: 301'd them to /blog in next.config.js instead of leaving them 404-bound or orphaned — preserves whatever link equity remains
J2.rationale: It made a real disposition (blanket 301 to /blog) with equity reasoning and cited aggregate ~15k impressions, which beats anchor_0, but it treated all 12 identically with no per-asset or tiered decisions grounded in individual impressions/positions — no harvest/repurpose of any salvageable post.

J3.basis: 301'd them... preserves whatever link equity remains rather than losing it outright. Seeded 4 new posts genuinely on-topic for Lumina... to start building topical authority in the new niche
J3.rationale: Cleanly avoids both failure modes — doesn't chase inbox content, doesn't torch equity — and builds the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine; but the bridge is a crude blanket redirect of topically-mismatched pages (soft-404 risk) with no sequencing or selective harvest, so the middle path is asserted more than engineered.

J1.basis: InboxZap (email cleanup tool) pivoted to Lumina (AI meeting assistant), but the migration was left half-done in code. GSC data showed *all 18 homepage clicks* were brand searches for "inboxzap"... while 12 legacy email-cleanup blog posts (still pulling ~15k impressions) had become orphaned
J1.rationale: Names the pivot explicitly, correctly reads the live site as the meeting assistant, the GSC history as legacy inbox demand, and the repo as half-migrated — and every fix (FAQ 'What happened to InboxZap?', Lumina cluster, retitled metadata) flows from that reality.

J2.basis: Retired the 12 off-topic legacy posts: 301'd them to /blog in next.config.js instead of leaving them 404-bound or orphaned — preserves whatever link equity remains
J2.rationale: It references the aggregate ~15k impressions and gives a real disposition (blanket 301) with equity reasoning, clearing anchor_0, but it treats all 12 identically and redirects to the blog index rather than walking the inventory asset-by-asset with per-post impressions/positions or routing each to a relevant Lumina successor or harvest decision.

J3.basis: 301'd them to /blog... preserves whatever link equity remains rather than losing it outright. Seeded 4 new posts genuinely on-topic for Lumina... to start building topical authority in the new niche
J3.rationale: Lands a defensible middle path — preserve legacy equity via redirect while building the meeting-assistant content cluster as the growth engine, and answers the still-converting brand query directly — though the transition is executed rather than sequenced as a staged plan, and redirecting to the index rather than topical successors leaks some of the equity it aims to preserve.
